description_long: This is a vintage computer keyboard with a rectangular, slightly sloped chassis and a full set of raised, round-edged keys. The casing is primarily a worn gray plastic, with visible scuffs and discoloration, suggesting significant use and age. The keyboard features a prominent label area on the upper right, which appears to display branding and model information, though the text is partially faded. The rear side has a series of ventilation slits and ports, likely for connectivity and cooling, and the overall build is robust and utilitarian. The keys are dark, contrasting with the lighter body, and one key in the upper right is highlighted in a different color, possibly for special function. The object is compact and portable, typical of early home or office computer peripherals.
description: A vintage, worn gray computer keyboard with raised dark keys and a prominent label area, showing signs of heavy use. The casing is robust with visible ports and ventilation on the back.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full key layout, label area, and the overall rectangular shape.
description_view_1: Left angled view highlighting the sloped profile and the side of the casing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying ventilation slits and connectivity ports, with visible wear on the casing.
description_view_3: Right angled view emphasizing the keyboard's depth and the raised key profile.
